author: Muhammad Hussain, Babu
title: Ek Musalman da Sadda, ya'ni ek Musalman ka paigham Sikh Bhaiyon ke nam
gloss: A message of a Musalman to his Sikh brethren, or a pamphlet trying to prove that Baba Nanak, the founder of the Sikh religion, was a true Muhammadan and that the Sikhs should follow their Guru.
publisher: M. Qasim Ali, Ahmadi, Proprietor ' Allaqq,'

author: Muhammad Ishaq, Mir
title: Islam aur Budh Mazhab
gloss: a comparison of some of the doctrines common to Buddhism and Muhammadanism in which the author tries to show that the latter religion is superior to the former.

author: Muhammad Jafar
title: Tazkirat-ul-Wa'izin
gloss: or an Urdu translation, by Hakim Zabir Ahmad Shah of an Arabic book of the same name, containing fifty-seven chapters on subjects useful to Muhummadan preachers.
publisher: Maliks Fazl Din, Chanan Din and Taj-ud-din, Booksellers
notes: [Previous edition noticed in entry No. 59 at page 44 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September 1908.]

author: Muhammad Sibtin, Sayad, Maulvi
title: Kashful Hijab
gloss: A pamphlet discussing the controversial subject whether Ali received his education from any man or through revelation, and whether Muhammad was able to read and write, before his becoming a prophet or after that time.

author: Muhammad Yusuf, Shaikh
title: Arya Dharm ka Photo
gloss: or a pamphlet directed against the Arya Samaj and Swami Dayanand its founder, quoting reference to their books.

flags.issue: publisher line partly reads 'ublished by the author'

author: Mukhtar Husain, Agha
title: A'ina-i-Islam
gloss: The Mirror of Islam, or a book describing the chief historical events from the time of the birth of Muhammad up to the martyrdom of Imam Husain, in the light of the Shiah faith.
publisher: Sayad Saghir Hasan, Proprietor, " Yusufi " Press

printer_verbatim: Newal Kishore Gas Printing Works, Lahore.
title: Muqaddas Kitab ka Ahwal, Hissa-i-Awwal
gloss: Translation of Births' Bible Stories, Part I.
